_meta.purpose: Human-friendly probable UI flow per Lambda — derived from handler source + serverless.yml triggers. For non-tech folks to spot-check on the actual product UI.

format.what_it_does: One sentence — what does this Lambda do?
format.trigger: What kicks it off in the real world (user action / scheduled / queue message).
format.ui_flow: Step-by-step from a user's POV. Where to click in Uniqode/Polo to make this Lambda run.
format.how_to_verify: What to look at on the UI to confirm it worked — e.g. 'check the connected sheet for a new row'.
format.external_systems: Third-party APIs the Lambda calls — these are NOT tested by Floci; require staging.
format.floci_tested: What Floci actually verifies (import safety, event parse, AWS calls).
format._note: Flows are PROBABLE based on code reading. Cross-check with PM if unsure.

forms-response-sync-service.what_it_does: Pushes a form response into the user's connected Google Sheet (or Microsoft Excel) row by row. Adds new columns automatically when a form gets new fields. Re-tries on Google API failures, gives up after 3 tries.
forms-response-sync-service.trigger: Someone submits a form on a Uniqode form page.
ui_flow: 1. In Uniqode dashboard → Forms → pick a form → Integrations tab → connect Google Sheets (one-time OAuth).
2. Note the spreadsheet that was connected.
3. Open the public form URL → fill it in → submit.
4. Wait ~5 seconds.
5. Open the connected Google Sheet.
how_to_verify: A new row appears at the bottom of the sheet with the answers you just submitted.
Column headers match the form fields (Name, Email, etc).
If you added a new field to the form after connecting, a NEW column should auto-appear in the sheet.
Submit again → second row appears below the first.
forms-response-sync-service.floci_tested: Lambda boots, parses the SQS message, can talk to AWS SQS for retries. The actual Google sync is NOT tested locally.

beaconstac_email_service.what_it_does: Sends transactional emails (signup confirmation, password reset, alert notifications, etc.) via AWS SES.
beaconstac_email_service.trigger: Backend pushes a message to SQS when an email needs to go out.
ui_flow: 1. Trigger any email-sending action — sign up a new user, click 'Forgot password', share a QR code via email, etc.
2. Wait ~10 seconds.
how_to_verify: Check the inbox of the recipient — email should arrive.
Check the email template renders correctly (logo, colors, links work).
If using AWS SES sandbox, sender/recipient must both be verified.
beaconstac_email_service.floci_tested: Boot + SQS event parse + SES API call (Floci has SES emulator — emails go to a fake mailbox, not real).

polo_email_service.what_it_does: Same as beaconstac_email_service but for The QR Code Generator (Polo) — separate stack, separate templates.
polo_email_service.trigger: Polo backend pushes a message to SQS for emails.
ui_flow: 1. Sign up on the-qrcode-generator.com or trigger any email action there.
2. Wait for delivery.
how_to_verify: Email arrives in inbox.
Branding shows 'The QR Code Generator', not Uniqode.
polo_email_service.floci_tested: Same as email_service — boot + parse + SES emulator call.

beaconstac_qr_code_generator.what_it_does: Generates a single QR code image (PNG/SVG/PDF) with the user's chosen design, logo, colors, frame.
beaconstac_qr_code_generator.trigger: User downloads or previews a QR code from the dashboard.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → QR Codes → Create / Edit a QR.
2. Customize design (colors, logo, frame).
3. Click Download → choose format (PNG / SVG / PDF) → click Download.
how_to_verify: QR image downloads in the chosen format.
Scan it with your phone camera → opens the destination URL.
Custom logo + colors render correctly.
beaconstac_qr_code_generator.floci_tested: Boot + image generation logic + S3 upload to Floci's local S3.

beaconstac_bulk_qr_generator.what_it_does: Generates many QR codes at once (e.g. 500 codes for a campaign) as a downloadable ZIP.
beaconstac_bulk_qr_generator.trigger: User clicks 'Bulk Generate' in the dashboard with a CSV of destinations.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → QR Codes → Bulk Create.
2. Upload a CSV with one URL per row.
3. Pick design template.
4. Click Generate → wait (can take minutes for thousands of codes).
how_to_verify: Email arrives with a download link OR the dashboard shows the bulk job as 'Ready'.
Download the ZIP → contains one image per row of your CSV.
Pick 3 random codes → scan them → each opens its corresponding URL.
external_systems: AWS ECS (the bulk generation runs in a container, this Lambda just kicks it off)
beaconstac_bulk_qr_generator.floci_tested: Boot + ECS RunTask call (Floci does NOT emulate ECS — call will fail locally).

beaconstac_pdf_compressor.what_it_does: Compresses PDFs uploaded to forms (so they don't bloat S3 / cost bandwidth). Has a sister function `mergePDF` that combines multiple PDFs into one.
beaconstac_pdf_compressor.trigger: User uploads a PDF as a form answer or attachment.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → Forms → form with a 'File Upload' field.
2. Open public form URL → upload a large PDF (>1MB) → submit.
3. Wait ~30 seconds.
how_to_verify: Open form responses → click on the uploaded PDF → it downloads.
Downloaded PDF is significantly smaller than the original (e.g. 5MB → 1MB).
The PDF still opens correctly in any reader.
external_systems: None (Ghostscript runs inside the Lambda container)
beaconstac_pdf_compressor.floci_tested: Boot + S3 GetObject + Ghostscript exec + S3 PutObject (all on Floci's local S3).

uniqode-print-qr-codes-pdf.what_it_does: Generates a printable PDF sheet of QR codes (e.g. 4-up, 8-up, 16-up layouts) for users to print on Avery labels.
uniqode-print-qr-codes-pdf.trigger: User clicks 'Print Sheet' in the dashboard.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → QR Codes → select multiple QRs.
2. Click 'Print' or 'Export' → choose a label sheet template.
3. Click Generate.
how_to_verify: PDF download starts within 30s.
PDF has correct number of QR codes per page.
Codes are sized to match a real Avery label sheet (print one to verify).
uniqode-print-qr-codes-pdf.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse + PDF generation + S3 upload (Floci-local).

beaconstac_image_optimizer.what_it_does: Resizes and compresses images uploaded by users (logos, profile pics, form attachments) to keep S3 storage low.
beaconstac_image_optimizer.trigger: User uploads any image to Uniqode.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → anywhere with an image upload (DBC profile pic, QR logo, link page background).
2. Upload a large image (>2MB).
3. Save.
how_to_verify: Image renders correctly on the page.
Inspect via browser dev tools → image URL → download → file is much smaller than what you uploaded.
beaconstac_image_optimizer.floci_tested: Boot + S3 GetObject + image resize + S3 PutObject.

beaconstac_profile_picture.what_it_does: Generates a default profile picture (avatar with initials) for new users who haven't uploaded one.
beaconstac_profile_picture.trigger: Scheduled job (runs nightly to backfill missing avatars).
ui_flow: 1. Sign up a new user without uploading a profile picture.
2. Wait until the next scheduled run (or trigger manually if you have admin access).
how_to_verify: User profile shows an avatar with their initials in a colored circle.
S3 has a generated PNG at the user's avatar URL.

beaconstac_svg_sanitizer.what_it_does: Strips dangerous content from user-uploaded SVG files (script tags, external image refs) before serving them. Security guard.
beaconstac_svg_sanitizer.trigger: User uploads an SVG (e.g. as a QR logo).
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → QR design → Logo → Upload SVG.
2. Upload any SVG.
3. Save the QR.
how_to_verify: SVG renders on the QR.
Open the served SVG URL → no `<script>` tags in source → no external `<image href>` refs.
beaconstac_svg_sanitizer.floci_tested: Boot + S3 GetObject + sanitize + S3 PutObject.

beaconstac_malware_detection.what_it_does: Checks every URL behind a QR code against a malware/phishing database to block bad actors using Uniqode for scams.
beaconstac_malware_detection.trigger: Scheduled (every few minutes) — scans recently-created QRs.
ui_flow: 1. Create a QR pointing to a known phishing URL (use a test URL from PhishTank or similar).
2. Wait ~10 minutes.
how_to_verify: Dashboard shows the QR as 'Flagged' or disabled.
Scanning the QR shows a warning page instead of redirecting.
external_systems: Google Safe Browsing API or similar threat feed
beaconstac_malware_detection.floci_tested: Boot + scheduled trigger handling + DDB read/write.

beaconstac_scheduler.what_it_does: Activates/deactivates scheduled QR codes — e.g. a QR set to 'live from Jan 1 to Mar 31' is enabled/disabled at the right time.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → create a QR with 'Schedule' enabled → set start date 2 minutes from now.
2. Save.
3. Wait until past the start time.
how_to_verify: QR scan resolves successfully after the start time.
Before start time → scan shows 'not active' page.

beaconstac_analytics.what_it_does: Sanitizes raw scan data before it lands in S3 for reporting (strips PII, normalizes timestamps).
beaconstac_analytics.trigger: Kinesis Firehose stream — every QR scan generates a record.
ui_flow: 1. Scan any Uniqode QR code with your phone.
2. Wait 5-10 minutes.
how_to_verify: Open the QR's Analytics tab → scan count incremented by 1.
Scan map shows your approximate location.

beaconstac_analytics_data_stream_to_fire.what_it_does: Pipes scan events into AWS Firehose for the analytics warehouse. Three variants: regular QR, link page, digital business card.
beaconstac_analytics_data_stream_to_fire.trigger: DynamoDB stream (every scan writes a row, this Lambda fires on the change).
ui_flow: 1. Scan any QR / open any link page / view any DBC.
2. Wait.
how_to_verify: Same as beaconstac_analytics — analytics dashboard count goes up.
beaconstac_analytics_data_stream_to_fire.floci_tested: Boot + DDB stream record parse + Firehose put.

beaconstac_analytics_pdf_generator.what_it_does: Generates the downloadable PDF of an analytics report.
beaconstac_analytics_pdf_generator.trigger: User clicks 'Export PDF' in analytics view.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → Analytics → pick any QR with scans.
2. Click 'Export' → 'PDF'.
3. Wait.
how_to_verify: PDF downloads.
Charts render correctly, colors match, scan counts match dashboard.
beaconstac_analytics_pdf_generator.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse + chart rendering + S3 PutObject.

beaconstac_analytics_validator.what_it_does: Internal QA tool — compares numbers between two analytics data sources to catch discrepancies in the warehouse.
ui_flow: Internal-only — not user-facing. Verify via internal dashboards / Datadog.
how_to_verify: Internal tooling check.

beaconstac_azure_integration_tunnel.what_it_does: Routes Uniqode requests through to Azure Active Directory (SSO/SAML) for enterprise customers.
beaconstac_azure_integration_tunnel.trigger: User logs in with Azure SSO.
ui_flow: 1. Use a tenant configured for Azure SSO.
2. Click 'Sign in with Azure' on Uniqode login.
3. Complete Azure auth flow.
how_to_verify: User lands in their Uniqode org dashboard.
Azure profile picture / name appears in account menu.
beaconstac_azure_integration_tunnel.floci_tested: Boot + HTTP event parse — actual Azure handshake NOT tested.

beaconstac_cloudfront_invalidator.what_it_does: Tells CloudFront CDN to flush cached versions of files when a user updates them (so changes show up immediately).
beaconstac_cloudfront_invalidator.trigger: Backend SQS message after a file is updated.
ui_flow: 1. Update a QR design / link page background image.
2. Save.
3. Refresh the public scan/landing page.
how_to_verify: Updated content appears immediately (not the cached old version).
If you see the old version → invalidation failed.
beaconstac_cloudfront_invalidator.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse — CloudFront API call NOT in Floci.

beaconstac_cloudfront_lambda@edge_handle.what_it_does: Runs at the CDN edge for every request — adds security headers, handles redirects, A/B test routing.
ui_flow: 1. Open any Uniqode public URL (scan a QR, view a link page).
2. Open browser dev tools → Network tab → click the request.
how_to_verify: Response headers include security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.).
Response time <100ms (edge function should be fast).
beaconstac_cloudfront_lambda@edge_handle.floci_tested: Boot — Lambda@Edge has special constraints (no env vars, etc.) Floci can't fully replicate.

beaconstac_custom_domain_ssl_handler.what_it_does: Handles SSL certificate provisioning when a customer adds their own domain (qr.acme.com → Uniqode).
beaconstac_custom_domain_ssl_handler.trigger: Backend SQS message after customer adds a custom domain.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → Settings → Custom Domains.
2. Add a domain (e.g. qr.testdomain.com).
3. Add the CNAME record in your DNS provider.
4. Wait ~5-15 minutes for verification.
how_to_verify: Dashboard shows the domain as 'Verified' / 'SSL Active'.
Visit https://yourdomain.com → shows valid SSL cert (no warning).
beaconstac_custom_domain_ssl_handler.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse — ACM + DNS NOT tested.

beaconstac_events_sender.what_it_does: Sends webhooks to customer-configured URLs when events happen (form submitted, QR scanned, etc.).
beaconstac_events_sender.trigger: Backend SQS message → first transformer reshapes data → sender Lambda fires the HTTP request.
ui_flow: 1. Uniqode dashboard → Settings → Webhooks → add a webhook URL (use webhook.site for testing).
2. Pick events (e.g. 'form.submitted').
3. Trigger the event (submit a form).
4. Wait ~5 seconds.
how_to_verify: On webhook.site → request appears with the event payload.
HTTP 200 in dashboard's webhook delivery log.
beaconstac_events_sender.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse + transform — actual HTTPS call NOT tested.

beaconstac_intercom_rate_limiter.what_it_does: Throttles outgoing requests to Intercom (customer support tool) to avoid hitting their rate limits.
beaconstac_intercom_rate_limiter.trigger: Backend SQS message — every customer event that needs to sync to Intercom.
ui_flow: 1. Sign up a new user.
2. Trigger many events quickly (set their plan, update their org, etc.).
how_to_verify: User appears in Intercom with all property updates.
Even after many rapid changes, no rate-limit errors in logs.
beaconstac_intercom_rate_limiter.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse + DDB rate-limit counter.

beaconstac_intercom_rate_limiter_polo.what_it_does: Same as above but for The QR Code Generator (Polo) traffic to Intercom.
ui_flow: Same flow as Uniqode side, but on the-qrcode-generator.com signups.
how_to_verify: Same — Intercom shows the user, no rate-limit errors.

beaconstac_ip_bot_scans_report.what_it_does: Daily report of suspicious IP addresses (bots scanning QR codes en masse) for the security team to review.
ui_flow: Internal tool — sends email/Slack to security team.
how_to_verify: Security team reviews the daily digest.

beaconstac_lead_scoring.what_it_does: Scores user accounts based on engagement metrics (logins, QRs created, forms built) to flag likely upgraders for sales.
ui_flow: Internal tool — sales team sees scores in their CRM, not the customer-facing UI.
how_to_verify: Score appears next to user in HubSpot/Salesforce.
beaconstac_lead_scoring.floci_tested: Boot — handler is currently commented out in serverless.yml.

beaconstac_product_scan_counter.what_it_does: Updates per-product scan counts (for product-tracking QRs) on a schedule.
ui_flow: 1. Create a Product QR → scan it 5 times.
2. Wait for next scheduled run.
how_to_verify: Product page shows scan count = 5.

beaconstac_publish_user_properties_ampli.what_it_does: Pushes user property updates (plan, role, team) to Amplitude for product analytics.
beaconstac_publish_user_properties_ampli.trigger: Backend SQS message on user property change.
ui_flow: 1. Update a user's plan/role/anything in admin panel.
2. Wait ~5 seconds.
how_to_verify: Amplitude → User Lookup → property updated.

beaconstac_publish_user_properties_fireb.what_it_does: Same as above but to Firebase (used by mobile apps).
beaconstac_publish_user_properties_fireb.trigger: Backend SQS message on user property change.
ui_flow: Update user property → check Firebase user properties.
how_to_verify: Firebase console → Authentication → user → custom claims updated.

ui_flow: 1. Dashboard → Reports → click Export.
2. Wait.
how_to_verify: File downloads, contents match dashboard data.

beaconstac_request_logs.what_it_does: Sanitizes incoming HTTP request logs (strips bearer tokens, cookies) before they go to S3.
beaconstac_request_logs.trigger: Kinesis Firehose stream of API requests.
ui_flow: Internal observability — not user-facing.
how_to_verify: Check S3 logs → no auth headers present.

beaconstac_scan_details.what_it_does: Generates the 'Scan Details' Excel/CSV when a user exports detailed scan history.
ui_flow: 1. Dashboard → Analytics → pick a QR with scans → click 'Export Detailed Scans'.
2. Wait.
how_to_verify: Excel file downloads with one row per scan, location/device/timestamp filled in.
beaconstac_scan_details.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse + Excel generation + S3 upload.

beaconstac_scans_map_view.what_it_does: Pre-aggregates scan data so the map view in analytics loads fast (no on-the-fly geo lookups).
ui_flow: 1. Scan a QR from a few different locations (or use VPN).
2. Wait for scheduled run.
3. Dashboard → Analytics → Map view.
how_to_verify: Map shows pins at all the locations you scanned from.

beaconstac_search.what_it_does: Indexes data into Elasticsearch so the dashboard's search bar (find a QR, find a user) works fast.
beaconstac_search.trigger: Kinesis stream — every CRUD operation on indexed entities.
ui_flow: 1. Create a new QR with a unique name.
2. Wait ~5 seconds.
3. Dashboard search bar → type the unique name.
how_to_verify: The new QR appears in search results.
beaconstac_search.floci_tested: Boot + Kinesis record parse — ES write NOT tested locally.

beaconstac_server_forms_app_sync.what_it_does: Syncs form definitions / responses between the main backend and the mobile-app forms service.
beaconstac_server_forms_app_sync.trigger: Backend SQS message on form change.
ui_flow: 1. Edit a form on the dashboard.
2. Open the Uniqode mobile app → Forms section.
how_to_verify: Mobile app shows the updated form.

beaconstac_webhook_service.what_it_does: The webhook execution engine — executes pending webhook deliveries and schedules retries on failure.
beaconstac_webhook_service.trigger: Two parts: a poll-scheduler (cron-like) finds due webhooks, executor sends them.
ui_flow: Same as beaconstac_events_sender — configure a webhook + trigger an event.
how_to_verify: webhook.site receives the call.
Failed webhooks retry with backoff.
beaconstac_webhook_service.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse + DDB schedule read/write.

beaconstac_zoominfo_enrichment.what_it_does: Enriches new signup user data with company info from ZoomInfo (industry, employees, revenue) for sales targeting.
ui_flow: Internal tool — sales sees enriched data in CRM.
how_to_verify: HubSpot/Salesforce contact has industry/company size populated.

cloudfront_analytics_dlq.what_it_does: Reprocesses CloudFront events that previously failed (dead-letter queue handler).
cloudfront_analytics_dlq.trigger: DLQ message from a failed earlier processing.
ui_flow: Internal — not user-facing.
how_to_verify: Failed events drain from DLQ; analytics counts catch up.

cloudwatch_slack.what_it_does: Posts CloudWatch alarms (e.g. 'API error rate >5%') to Slack channels for on-call engineers.
ui_flow: Internal — engineering only.
how_to_verify: #alerts Slack channel receives a message when an alarm fires.

email_reports.what_it_does: Generates and emails weekly analytics reports for QR / DBC / Polo customers (separate handlers per product).
ui_flow: 1. Wait until Monday morning.
2. Check inbox of any user who has 'Weekly Report' enabled.
how_to_verify: Email arrives with last-week's scan/view counts and charts.

gen-ai-qr-codes.what_it_does: Generates artistic AI-styled QR codes (the 'AI QR' feature) — uses an image diffusion model.
gen-ai-qr-codes.trigger: User clicks 'Generate AI QR' in the dashboard.
ui_flow: 1. Dashboard → QR Codes → Create → AI Style.
2. Pick a theme prompt (e.g. 'cyberpunk neon').
3. Click Generate.
4. Wait 20-60 seconds.
how_to_verify: Image renders with the chosen style.
Scanning it still resolves the destination URL.
Generate twice with same prompt → results differ slightly (random seed).
external_systems: AI image generation API (likely Replicate or similar)
gen-ai-qr-codes.floci_tested: Boot + SQS parse — actual diffusion call NOT tested.

scan_guard_daily_email_digest.what_it_does: Daily digest of suspicious scan activity to QR owners (e.g. 'we blocked 50 bot scans on your code yesterday').
ui_flow: 1. Have a QR with active 'Scan Guard' setting on.
2. Wait until next morning.
how_to_verify: Email arrives with digest of blocked attempts.

third-eye-webhook-consumer.what_it_does: Receives webhooks from a third-party service (Third Eye — likely a fraud/security partner).
third-eye-webhook-consumer.trigger: External HTTP POST from Third Eye.
ui_flow: Internal integration — admin tools only.
how_to_verify: Admin dashboard reflects the incoming data.

third-eye-webhook-data-transformer.what_it_does: Reshapes Third Eye webhook payloads before storing them.
third-eye-webhook-data-transformer.trigger: DynamoDB stream after consumer writes the raw event.
ui_flow: Internal — pipeline step.
how_to_verify: Transformed records appear in target store.

una_ai_chat_data_stream_to_firehose.what_it_does: Pipes Uniqode's AI chat (Una) sessions and messages into the analytics warehouse.
ui_flow: 1. Use the in-product AI chat (Una) — ask it any question.
2. Wait for analytics rollup.
how_to_verify: Chat usage shows up in internal analytics dashboards.

uniqode_anomaly_synthetic_data_generator.what_it_does: Generates synthetic 'anomalous' scan data for testing anomaly-detection models. Internal QA tool.
ui_flow: Internal QA — not customer-facing.
how_to_verify: Synthetic data appears in the dev analytics store.

wallet_pass_email_sender.what_it_does: Emails Apple Wallet / Google Wallet pass files (.pkpass) to users who request them (e.g. event ticket QR).
wallet_pass_email_sender.trigger: Backend SQS message after wallet pass is generated.
ui_flow: 1. Create a QR with 'Wallet Pass' option enabled.
2. Public scan page → click 'Add to Wallet'.
3. Provide an email address.
4. Wait ~10 seconds.
how_to_verify: Email arrives with .pkpass attachment.
Tap on iPhone → opens in Apple Wallet.

webpage-parser-service.what_it_does: Scrapes / parses webpages — likely to extract Open Graph data when a user enters a URL (for link previews on link pages).
webpage-parser-service.trigger: Backend call when a URL is added.
ui_flow: 1. Dashboard → Link Pages → add a link block → paste a URL.
2. Wait ~3 seconds.
how_to_verify: The link block auto-fills with title, description, thumbnail from that URL.
external_systems: The target webpage being scraped (any HTTPS site)
webpage-parser-service.floci_tested: Boot + S3 read/write — actual HTTP scrape NOT tested.

weekly_analytics_mailer.what_it_does: Older variant of email_reports — likely being deprecated. Sends weekly analytics emails.
ui_flow: Same as email_reports — check inbox Monday morning.
how_to_verify: Email arrives with weekly stats.
